title: Add regression coverage for historical compatibility and malformed persistence
goal: Add regression coverage that proves documented historical persistence formats remain replayable under bounded compatibility handling, while malformed, ambiguous, or non-deterministically replayable persistence fails explicitly and does not degrade into healthy canonical state.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 redesign keeps historical data readable only within a bounded compatibility policy and explicitly rejects malformed persistence as a silent fallback path. This ticket turns that boundary into executable regression coverage so future changes cannot reintroduce heuristic recovery, silent normalization, or compatibility behavior that weakens the new canonical protocol.
scope: Add tests for documented historical persistence formats that should remain readable and deterministically replayable.
Add tests for malformed materialized state, malformed event logs, malformed issuance metadata, and ambiguous persistence combinations that must fail explicitly.
Add tests that distinguish missing artifacts from malformed artifacts where the plan requires different behavior.
Add tests that confirm compatibility handling is bounded to historical read/rebuild behavior and does not redefine new-write semantics.
Add tests that confirm compatibility behavior does not reintroduce ticket-derived tx_id logic, sentinel active-transaction semantics, timestamp-first recovery, or planning-artifact-based recovery.
Add tests that confirm exact active transaction identity is preserved across compatibility-aware rebuild paths when historical data is still deterministically replayable.
out_of_scope: Changing runtime compatibility behavior itself beyond what is required to make tests pass.
Changing the canonical materialized continuation contract.
Broad unrelated test refactors outside compatibility and malformed-persistence behavior.
inputs: Outputs from p2-t01, p2-t02, p2-t03, p2-t04, and p2-t08
Existing test harnesses and persistence-related runtime modules
expected_outputs: A regression test set covering replayable historical formats and explicit malformed-persistence failures.
A compatibility-versus-malformed test matrix covering materialized state, event log, issuance metadata, and active transaction recovery behavior.
A short test-oriented note describing which historical cases are intentionally supported and which malformed cases intentionally fail.

historical_compatibility_cases: Historical transaction ID formats that are still documented as replayable.
Historical active-transaction representations that remain deterministically interpretable under bounded compatibility handling.
Historical session-oriented metadata that may still be readable without becoming the primary correctness anchor.
Historical event-log shapes that can still be replayed deterministically under the documented policy.
malformed_failure_cases: Ambiguous persistence that prevents safe exact active-transaction recovery.
Ordering or replay conditions that cannot be deterministically interpreted.
boundary_cases: Missing event log versus present-but-empty event log.
Missing tx_id counter metadata versus malformed tx_id counter metadata.
Historical compatibility normalization on read paths versus prohibited normalization in new-write behavior.
Compatibility-aware rebuild preserving exact active tx_id versus invalid recovery attempting to mint or infer a replacement transaction.

rules_to_validate: Documented historical formats may remain readable only if they are deterministically replayable under bounded compatibility handling.
Malformed, ambiguous, or non-deterministically replayable persistence must fail explicitly.
Compatibility handling must not silently reinterpret invalid persistence as healthy canonical state.
Missing and malformed artifacts must remain distinguishable where the plan defines different handling.
Compatibility handling must not weaken exact active-transaction continuation.
Compatibility handling must not reintroduce string-shape heuristics, sentinel IDs, timestamp-first selection, or planning artifacts as canonical runtime truth.
Compatibility handling must be read-path-only or rebuild-path-only where applicable and must not redefine 0.6.0 new-write semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Regression coverage exists for all documented historical compatibility cases identified as still replayable under the plan.
Regression coverage exists for malformed persistence cases that must fail explicitly.
Tests distinguish missing event logs from malformed event logs and missing issuance metadata from malformed issuance metadata where the plan requires that distinction.
Tests prove that compatibility handling cannot override the minimal canonical continuation contract when healthy materialized state is available.
Tests prove that compatibility-aware rebuild preserves exact active transaction identity when a historical case is still replayable.
Tests prove that malformed or ambiguous persistence does not degrade into inferred healthy state or replacement transaction selection.
Tests prove that compatibility support does not weaken strict 0.6.0 new-write semantics.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that every test case is justified by a specific compatibility or malformed-persistence rule in the plan.
Confirm that accepted historical cases are documented and bounded rather than open-ended.
Confirm that failing cases are truly malformed or ambiguous and not merely unsupported convenience shapes.
Confirm that exact active tx_id continuity remains preserved in every compatibility-aware success path.
Confirm that no regression test accidentally encodes obsolete heuristic recovery as desired behavior.
notes: This ticket should encode the boundary between legitimate historical support and prohibited heuristic recovery.
Prefer explicit negative tests for malformed or ambiguous persistence rather than assuming the absence of compatibility behavior is sufficient.
If a historical format is kept replayable, the test should state why it is still deterministic and why it does not weaken the new canonical protocol.
